title: Interop: Global Symbols import / OBF round-trip validation
context: For open-pictograms to be part of the open AAC ecosystem, it must interoperate with Global Symbols (the largest open symbol reference database) and confirm that its OBF export round-trips cleanly through OBF-consuming tools (e.g. CoughDrop-style board builders). This task implements a Global Symbols import adapter (to pull in or cross-reference Concept Coding IDs for interop), validates the OBF round-trip, and documents confirmed interoperability with at least one external ecosystem.
objective: Implement the Global Symbols import adapter and OBF round-trip validation, confirming interoperability with at least one external AAC ecosystem; publish the interop evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Global Symbols / Concept Coding ID cross-references are populated in interopRefs metadata fields for at least the core communication vocabulary (batch-001 symbols); the mapping logic is documented.

OBF round-trip validation: an OBF file exported by the pipeline is imported into at least one OBF-consuming tool or validator; the round-trip result (import success, any data loss or format issues) is documented.
Adapter lives in adapters/ (per Elyos architecture); contains no vendor-specific logic outside the adapter directory; is TypeScript/ESM, pnpm-based, passes pnpm build && pnpm lint, licensed MIT.
Interop evidence document confirms compatibility with ≥1 external ecosystem; any format gaps or compatibility issues are recorded with remediation notes.
PR includes the adapter code, interop evidence document, and updated metadata records with interopRefs populated.
resources: Global Symbols / Open Symbols / Concept Coding — open AAC interoperability ecosystem
Open Board Format (OBF) — open AAC board interchange format
output: adapters/global-symbols/ (Global Symbols import adapter), docs/interop-evidence.md (round-trip validation results), and updated metadata records with interopRefs; all licensed MIT (code) and CC-BY-4.0 (docs/data). PR opened against main.
